name: wcag-accessibility-audit
description: Audits a web project against WCAG 2.2 and returns a prioritized fix list. Works in three passes: a source scan that flags code-level problems (images without alt text, inputs without labels, focus outlines removed, ~50 patterns across HTML/CSS/JS/React/Vue/Svelte); a runtime check via @axe-core/playwright or Chrome DevTools MCP Lighthouse when available, catching real contrast ratios and runtime-resolved ARIA that source scanning can't see; and a manual-review checklist for things no tool can evaluate (keyboard flow, screen reader announcements, clear error messaging). Each finding is tied to a specific WCAG success criterion with severity, file and line, affected user groups, and a fix example. Reports land at docs/accessibility/wcag-audit-YYYY-MM-DD.md. The plugin understands React/Next.js, Tailwind, MUI, Radix, and Headless UI, and knows which of those inject ARIA automatically, so elements that look unlabeled in source but work at runtime don't show up as findings. Use before launch, for ADA/Section 508 reviews, or when chasing reported accessibility issues.
